data.current_description: Creates a new version for the specified site.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Version for the specified site. A Version represents a configuration and collection of static files that determine how the site is displayed. The request body should contain the Version object with desired configuration. Returns the newly created Version object with a unique version identifier. This operation prepares content for deployment but does not make it publicly available until released.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description is too brief and lacks context about what a Version is, what inputs are expected, what is returned, and the relationship to the deployment workflow. Added domain context about Versions, clarified that this is a preparatory step before release, and explained the return value for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'firebasehosting.sites.versions.clone' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Missing summary. Description lacks clarity about what CloneVersionRequest contains, what exactly is cloned (files and configuration), what is returned, and practical use cases. Enhanced to explain the request structure, scope of cloning operation, return value, and use cases to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Creates a new version on the specified target site using the content of the specified version.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Version on the specified target site by cloning the content and configuration from a source version. The CloneVersionRequest specifies the source version to copy from. This operation duplicates all static files and configuration settings from the source version to create an identical version on the target site. Returns the newly created Version object on the target site. Useful for promoting content across multiple sites or creating backups.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description lacks clarity about what CloneVersionRequest contains, what exactly is cloned (files and configuration), what is returned, and practical use cases. Enhanced to explain the request structure, scope of cloning operation, return value, and use cases to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'firebasehosting.sites.versions.populateFiles' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Missing summary. Description only mentions file size constraint but lacks context about what files are being added, what the request/response contain, and the operational workflow. Added clarity about file types supported, request/response structure, the 2 GB per-file constraint context, and the relationship to the release workflow for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Adds content files to the specified version. Each file must be under 2 GB.
data.description_suggestion: Adds static content files to the specified Version. The PopulateVersionFilesRequest contains the files to be uploaded, with each individual file limited to 2 GB. Files are associated with the target version and can include HTML, CSS, JavaScript, images, and other static assets. Returns a PopulateVersionFilesResponse confirming the files added to the version. This operation must be completed before the version can be released to serve content.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description only mentions file size constraint but lacks context about what files are being added, what the request/response contain, and the operational workflow. Added clarity about file types supported, request/response structure, the 2 GB per-file constraint context, and the relationship to the release workflow for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Creates a new release, which makes the content of the specified version actively display on the appropriate URL(s).
data.summary_suggestion: Create a release to deploy content
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new release that deploys the specified version's content to the live URL(s). This operation makes the version's configuration and files publicly accessible. Requires the parent site or channel resource path and a Release object specifying the version to deploy. Returns the created Release object. Side effect: The specified version becomes the active release and its content is immediately served to end users.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description explains the effect but lacks context about required inputs, return value, and explicit side effects.
